rulings.Case Title: 1355 MARKET STREET ASSOCIATES LP A LIMITED et al VS. PLANNING DEPARTMENT OF THE CITY AND COUNTY OF et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, JULY 13, 2007, LINE 8. REAL-PARTY-IN-INTEREST MERCY HOUSING CALIFORNIA, MERCY HOUSING CALIFORNIA XIV'S DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION: The demurrer is overruled; defendants are to answer within 10 days of service of the order. The demurrer raises multiple issues that are best addressed by subsequent motions based on admissible evidence. The Petition states sufficient facts that counter respondents' contentions. · The Court takes judicial notice of Lakritz' Exhibits A, A-1, A-2, C, C-1 and D. · Lack of standing is based on the failure to object to CEQA findings. However, the complaint pleads lack of notice. (Paragraphs 15-19.) Taking into account the documents judicially noticed, the court cannot decide, as a matter of law, that Petitioner had notice that the sidewalk would be extended 5 feet into Jessie Street. Moreover, the argument made in support of a lack of standing is contrary to the argument made in challenging the nuisance claim. · Exhaustion of administrative remedies is based on notice and for the same reason, the demurrer is overruled. · It appears that the applicable limitations period is 180 days. However, the statute either did not begin to run and/or was tolled given the uncertainty of the proposal to reduce the width of Jessie Street. (Paragrahps 15-20.) · The Petition alleges imminent harm and an intent to ignore City regulations; those allegations negate the respondents' contentions that the nuisance claims fail. =(302/PJM/KD)
